---
title: "Quarto Workshop"
subtitle: "Write reproducible scientific documents — one source, many outputs"
lang: en
toc: false
---

> 🌐 **Versi Bahasa Indonesia:** [baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia](../index.qmd)

Welcome! This workshop introduces **[Quarto](https://quarto.org)** — a scientific
publishing system that unites **prose + code + data** in a single file and renders to
**PDF, HTML, Word, and slides** from one source.

## Who is this for?

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🎓 Students
Writing a quantitative **thesis/dissertation**: data analysis, tables, figures,
citations, and a campus-formatted PDF — without copy-pasting from SPSS/Excel into Word.
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🔬 Lecturers & Researchers
Producing **reproducible papers, reports, and presentations**. Data changed? Just
re-render — numbers, tables, figures, and slides update automatically.
:::
:::

## The problem we solve

| The old way (Word + SPSS/Excel) | With Quarto |
|---|---|
| Numbers copied by hand → typos | Numbers appear **automatically** from code |
| Table/figure numbering breaks on insert | Numbering & table of contents **automatic** |
| Revising data = redo analysis + re-paste | Change data → **re-render** once |
| Manual citations & bibliography | Automatic citations (e.g. APA), via Zotero |
| Slides rebuilt separately | Slides from the **same data** (Beamer/Reveal.js) |

## The core idea

```
  .qmd file   ──►  Quarto  ──►  PDF · HTML · Word · Slides
 (prose +         (run the      (clean, reproducible
  code + data)     code, weave)   output)
```

**One source, many outputs.** Change one thing in the data, and **everything** updates.
That's *reproducibility* — and we'll prove it live today.

## Get started

- 👉 **[Follow the hands-on tutorial](hands-on.qmd)** — step by step, do it yourself.
- 🖥️ **[Download the slides (PDF/Beamer)](../slides/slides-en.pdf)**
- 📘 **Full book "Quarto untuk Skripsi" (Indonesian):**
  <https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/>

::: {.callout-tip}
## What to install
**R + RStudio** (RStudio ships with Quarto), **TinyTeX** for PDF, and **optionally
Python** for the Python examples. Details in the [tutorial](hands-on.qmd#sec-setup).
:::
